title: Transform Job, Row Patch, And Provider Receipt
purpose: Contract for low-authority provider work: a controller-owned transform_job consumes projection packets, a provider returns structured candidate JSON, the harness persists a provider_receipt, and any proposed row change remains a draft row_patch until controller promotion.

storage_contract.rule: Provider workers write only compute-worker state artifacts. They do not edit source, standards, doctrine, or generated projections directly.

transform_job.rule: A transform_job must materialize projection inputs inside input_packet. It may point to source fingerprints for audit, but the provider prompt should not require raw repo reads. Every transform_job carries local_evidence_override_policy: source memos, continuation notes, dispatch contours, and source packets are proposed contours; local system evidence in the packet and governing artifacts wins when they conflict.

row_patch.rule: A row_patch is candidate substrate. Promotion requires deterministic validation and controller review.

navigation_cluster_contract.purpose: Keep row_patches browseable without expanding every draft patch body into first-contact context.
navigation_cluster_contract.why_stable: target_facet is a required row_patch field and names the destination facet being proposed for controller review.
navigation_cluster_contract.authority_boundary: cluster_flag is a contents page over durable row_patch state; state/compute_workers/row_patches remains source projection and controller promotion remains separate.

provider_receipt.rule: Every attempted provider transform writes a receipt, including rejected, cached, duplicate-blocked, and schema-failed runs.

navigation_cluster_contract.purpose: Keep provider receipts browseable without expanding every receipt, provider metadata body, source fingerprint list, or validation result into first-contact context.
navigation_cluster_contract.why_stable: task_class is a required provider_receipt field and names the transform family being attempted.
navigation_cluster_contract.authority_boundary: cluster_flag is a contents page over durable provider receipt state; state/compute_workers/receipts remains source projection and controller promotion remains separate.

budget_contract.openrouter_rule: Automatic OpenRouter transform jobs remain free-only unless a specific job carries an explicit operator-approved paid budget.
budget_contract.retry_rule: The harness records failed run fingerprints and blocks uncontrolled duplicate reruns unless force is explicit.

annex_patterns.pattern: shared OpenAI-compatible provider adapter plus proactive/reactive rate-limit envelope
single provider factory and normalized content contract across OpenAI-compatible providers
atomic writes, wake gates, and local receipts around scheduled low-cost work
thin mutable harness with one primitive layer rather than a second framework
